text.title: Python Basics — Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when python-basics is mis-applied, each with symptom, root cause, and concrete fix.

antipattern.reason: Root cause: Same list shared across every call — silently accumulates state. Fix: Default to None and create the list inside the function body.
Root cause: TOCTOU race on dynamic attributes; verbose; slower than try/except on happy path. Fix: try: obj.x except AttributeError: ...
Root cause: Swallows KeyboardInterrupt, MemoryError, debug-time bugs; hides root causes. Fix: Catch the narrowest exception you can handle; let everything else propagate.

antipattern.description: from typing import List, Optional, Dict.
antipattern.reason: Root cause: Adds an import for no benefit; not the 3.12+ idiom; mypy strict treats them as legacy. Fix: Use list, dict, X | None directly.
Root cause: No structural type; typos in keys; no IDE autocomplete. Fix: Use @dataclass(frozen=True) with explicit field types.
